Rerekapa Track
trails.description: This track was made around 1910 by road workers using wheelbarrows, picks and shovels.

trails.name: Leitch's Track
trails.description: This track leads to Leitch's clearing and Leitch's Hut.

trails.name: Mahoenui Track
trails.description: Walk alongside the Awakino River to Leitch's Hut.

trails.name: Mangapohue Natural Bridge Walk
trails.description: Walk through an impressive limestone gorge that passes underneath a 17 m high natural arch.

trails.name: Mangatoa Track
trails.description: Crossing the beautiful Herangi Range, this alternative track leads to Leitch's Hut.

trails.name: Mount Messenger to Whitecliffs Walkway Track
trails.description: This track climbs to the summit of Mt Messenger and then descends to the coast.

trails.name: Rerekapa Track
trails.description: This track was made around 1910 by road workers using wheelbarrows, picks and shovels.

trails.name: Tawarau Forest tramping tracks
trails.description: The limestone features and native vegetation in the Tawarau Forest can best be seen by walking on one of the forest's scenic tracks.

trails.name: Te Kauri Park Circuit Walk
trails.description: Walk alongside the attractive Waikuku Stream then climb up to good views of the surrounding countryside.

trails.name: Waikawau Track
trails.description: This route for intrepid trampers is the longest track to Leitch's Hut.

trails.name: Whitecliffs Walkway
trails.description: This walkway uses the Kapuni to Auckland gas pipeline route for a considerable distance.  The laying of the pipeline opened up walking access to the dramatic forest and coastal country of the region.

trails.name: Moki Track
trails.description: The track was developed with wheelbarrows, picks and shovels as early as 1910. Both ends of the track pass through some 3 km of farmland before entering the forest, which contains good stands of tawa, kamahi and rimu.

trails.name: Mount Damper Falls Walk
trails.description:
At 74 m high, Mount Damper Falls are one of the highest in the North Island.

From the car park step over the style and walk over the formed track beside the creek. The first 10 minutes are over open farmland - respect private property, leave gates as you find them and stay on the track. Follow the track until you reach the bridge, from where the bush starts. The track descends gradually to two viewing platforms..

trails.name: Rerekapa Track
trails.description: This track was made around 1910 by road workers using wheelbarrows, picks and shovels.

trails.name: Whitecliffs Walkway
trails.description: Be prepared for changes in the weather. IIf walking along the beach, check tide times. Get local advice on track and weather conditions. Take warm clothes, including hat, gloves and waterproof raincoat.

trails.name: Mount Damper to Te Rerepahupahu Falls track
trails.description: This track to Te Rerepahupahu Falls is recommended for fit and experienced trampers only.

trails.name: Waitaanga South Road to Te Rerepahupahu Falls Track
trails.description: Follow an historic logging tramline to Te Rerepahupahu Falls.
